line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Quote Ref:

Description of changes:
We had a site meeting at Marsden and require updates to the drawings if possible.

Appreciate and our last issue we have p1a which is still required but not shown on the markup attached. 

MC 03 – MC base not to clash with the entrance door to the amenity space.
MC 04 – Move right unit to edge of the scaffold gantry. Right wing added with deck extensions into the hoist location. Hoist now moved.
MC 05 – Sow 2 board extension to whole left wing.
MC 06 – No change. Leave originally shown
MC 07 – No change. Leave as shown
MC 08 – Swap to Alher 130 and reduce to 12m. Right wing needs 2 board extensions.
MC 09 – Swap to Alher 120 twin and extend to left an extra 2m to the opening.
MC 10 – Increase MC to 14.5m to suit internal room layout.
MC 11 – As MC 10 has increased, MC 11 is to be reduced accordingly.
MC 13 – Swap to previous issue with the single Alher 130.
 All positions will now require roll out decks fitting to the platforms that do not have extensions fitted.

Thanks Derek, Could these be updated asap please mate

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Description of changes:

Reduce MCWP 07 by a 1.5m deck and increase MCWP 08 by a 1.5m deck,  meaning the RHS mast of MCWP 07 will move approx. 1.5m towards gridline 9 and the LHS mast of MCWP 08 will move approx. 1.5m onto or around gridline 6. Mark-up attached.

Change MCWP 03 to an Alba 120 with a working platform length of 26.9m, meaning MCW P02 will be reduced accordingly. Please configure to suit. Mark-up attached.

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Description of changes:
P9 / P10 – Add dim for the ties from grid.

P9 – Had to lower the top tie to the steel below and added a raker tie to aid the over sail.

On our base loadings we have a sleeper size showing 200mm x 95mm. Site are supplying sleepers that are 240mm x 120mm. Can you amend this sleeper size for these two positions please.

On p6, p7 and p8 our ops team were unable to put the first tie in the original position as the brickwork had been built already. Ops have gone ahead and tied to a C-Channel that is a little higher using half couplers (image below). Can you do a separate tie detail for this too please.

line_items.description:
Title: Perimeter Access with Roof plus internals
Grid Lines: NA

3D Model:  Yes

System:
Tube & Fitting


Load Class:
BS EN 1991-1-4 Environmental Loads
BS EN 13374 Edge Protection Loads
General Purpose (2.00kN/m2)


Cladding:
Sheeting

Ties:
To brickwork facade


Scope

External Scaffold
Gantry at the front elevation incorporating a lifting beam to run from the road all the way into the building via the windows
tower to the LHS to allow big spine beam to run front to back
Tower at rear to take the spine beam at the back
Scaffold to the front elevation above the gantry
Scaffold to the RHS elevation off the little alley way
Bridged scaffold to the rear
Temporary roof scaffold (mono pitch) above the existing roof structure leaning towards the little alley
 

Internal Scaffold
Independent scaffold to three sides for wall repairs (likely back‑propped down to ground, or with slab openings for standards to pass through to ground level)
Birdcage scaffold beneath the roof with a table top lift for roof repair works (this will effectively be a bridged platform between the internal wall independents noted above)
Crash deck to the underside of the first‑floor slab to allow full slab removal

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Quote Ref:

Description of changes:
On Site @ B&K PP2 we have a base that the client needs to span a manhole and utilities trench (right hand base of Pos 08).
I have suggested that we have 2No. circa 2m steels running away from the building with then a circa 7.2m steel spanning across these.
The 2mtr Steel will be sat on a concrete pad we can bolt the steels down to with he 7.2mtr stee then ‘bridging’ these (connected to the 2mtr steel as / if required).
Can you 1) Confirm feasibility and 2) Issue a quote for the full design of this detail (if feasible)

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Description of changes:
We have a slight issue on this project on the North Elevation. The scaffolders cannot lower the gantry due to this elevation being on a slope and would not leave enough head room for the public under p14, p15, p16. Meaning the base frame is actually level with where we would need to tie at base mast.

 

Speaking internally could you generate a tie detail where we use 2 wall plates attached to the steel beam and then connect these together with a tube and 2 half couplers. 1 half couple on each wall plate. We would then need to raker of the tube upwards but onto the 2nd mast section as we are unable to tie to the base mast as this cannot be fitted with a tie frame. Hopefully, this isn’t too steep and does work.

 

Also, the elevation drawing would need a slight adjustment to work at the correct height as the scaffold drawing attached.

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Quote Ref:

Description of changes:
Please can you update p1, p2, p3, and p4 as attached please. Have change the machine type to Alher 130s due to the customer not been happy with the number of ties on this elevation. With the courtyard though we are still using BFT 90s as this must be the same due to roof loadings.

Note slightly reduced over sail and top ties removed from both elevations as well please

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Quote Ref:

Description of changes:
On the elevations with balconies, the stubs protrude 590mm from the slab edge. Platform handrails to be 740mm from slab edge with a 450mm front extension which will also profile the stubs (reduced to 225mm during the project). This refers to Positions 9, 13, 17, 18, 21 and 25.

Stand off for all other positions at 550mm from slab to handrails (as already shown).

Positions 3, 4, 5 & 27 will be based on a load bearing scaffold at level 1.

Positions 14, 15 and 17 are all based from a second floor roof.

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Description of changes:
The client on this one suddenly realised that we had to locate the mast ties in the window locations, and after a lot of back and forth, the attached drawing is now the up-to-date design for this one.

There are several changes. Most of the roof positions have been removed (other than P20 & P21 which are now BFT90’s). The positions and configurations have changed. The starting level of some positions have changed. I’ve also attached a tie location mark-up that I did a couple of weeks ago which gives the base levels and the windows where the masts line up with. If there are any discrepancies between the mark-up and the plan, please follow the plan drawing.

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Quote Ref: 3680 - Hoist run-off

Email request dated: 14-02-26

Description of changes:
1. Hoist mast to be behind the hoist as per mark up attached as loading/unloading can only be achieved from one side.
2. Crashdeck below hoist to bring it level with the slab as there is a drop
3. Independent and Hoist to go all the way to the roof with run offs at window level (please see attached picture) 4. Propping to be erected to underside of slab at floor level as show where acrows but will require to be tube & fitting and to go into basement area as well (Subject to loadings)
5. Propping within basement area to full underside of craskdeck/Hoist area.

line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Description of changes:
Morning Derek,

We require an additional position adding to Marsden House if possible.

Where position 1 is situated this needs to be broken into 2 positions.

P1a- single added into the left corner to get full height on the elevation.

P1 – Can this be made into a small twin if the units miss the doors ways. If not, it will need to be a large single Alher 130.

P12 – R/H wing may need to be adapted to suit the new p1a

Can all drawings be updated and reissued please.

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Description of changes:
Hi Derek,
We have been going back and for the with site on this project and believe we have come to a conclusion on the layout – see attached. See below a few notes on each position;
Position 2 – Single BFT130 – Double Stacked
Position 3 – Single BFT130 – Double Stacked with wrap around deck extensions
Position 4 – Twin BFT130 – Double Stacked
Position 5A – Single BFT130 – Single Stacked with wrap around deck extensions
Position 6 – Single BFT130 – Single Stacked – With wrap around deck extensions
Position 7 – Single BFT130 – Single Stacked – On Load Bearing Scaffold
Position 8 – Twin BFT130 – Double Stacked
Position 11 – Single BFT130 – Double Stacked
Position 12 – Single BFT130 – Double Stacked – Will require adaption at level 8 to remove 800mm deck on right wing. Once up to level 8
Position 13 – Single BFT130 – Double Stacked – Coming from Level 8 on cantilevered scaffold – with wrap around deck extensions
Position 14 – Single BFT130 – Double Stacked – Coming from Level 8 on cantilevered scaffold
Position 15 – Single BFT130 – Double Stacked – Coming from Level 8 on cantilevered scaffold

Please can you also ensure that all ties are no more than 6m apart for the double stacked positions.
If all drawings could be updated to sui, it would be greatly appreciated.

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Description of changes:

Thanks for your work on this, the scaffold design is / was now as pretty much required.

However, the client has now subsequently asked up to move away from this solution and they will instead install a concrete pad at ground level, for us to fix a bespoke base steel base detail, similar to the attached you have completed on another project.

On that basis, can you proceed with a steel base detail and a raker tie back to slab for 2No. single positions (D16 & D17) configured as below

Dwayne

Please proceed with the small base bespoke design. From previous experience I do know this should be approx. 1120 x1190 x 200mm deep. (see extract from previous Temp Works Design brief from a previous project).

I will need your design with loads to get the base designed by our Temporary Works Engineer.

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:
Additional time required due to change of scope for the quote referenced below:

Description of changes: 

Hi Darren,

Thanks for your time earlier on.

I’ve had a quick look at the design packs and noted the following:

Commercial Building – Mast Climber 7a

Latest revision: C04

I am missing the loading onto the foundation (in this case, onto the L02 slab). This information was included in the previous P05 revision but does not appear to be provided in the C04 pack.

Hotel – Mast Climber 10a

Latest revision: C03

I am missing the loading onto the foundation (in this case, onto L01), and the specific drawing showing the tie loading and configuration.

In addition, for Mast Climber 10a (BFT 120 ALHER), drawing BFT263-DWG-M-0143-C03 indicates that the top tie is located 7.5 m above the tie below. However, the instruction manual states a maximum tie spacing of 6 m?

Based on this, I believe the ties might be at Levels 02, 04, 06, and 08? (see screenshot below from the model). There is also an additional floor level above this, up to roof level.

line_items.name: - Additional Design Required
line_items.description:

Could you provide a quotation to make these two changes please.
